name: Grandmas Sticky Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dough using the specified ingredients.
2. Cut the dough into 12 to 15 equal pieces and roll each into a rope about 6 to 8 inches long, tie in a knot, and pinch the ends together. Place the pinched end side down in a lightly greased pan. Let rise until doubled, about 45 minutes.
3. Alternatively, cut the dough into 36 equal pieces and form into balls. Place three balls into a muffin tin. Let rise as previously stated.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. Bake for 13 to 15 minutes, or until the tops are golden brown.
6. For the sticky topping, melt butter and sugar together in a pan. Allow the mixture to cool slightly before brushing it onto the rolls.
7. These rolls are delicious plain; they also reheat well. If you prefer them less browned, bake at 250°F (121°C) for 20 to 30 minutes. Do not brown further. Let cool completely before storing.
8. To store, keep in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days or freeze for up to 1 month.
9. For cinnamon rolls, roll the dough into a rectangle about 1/4 to 1/8 inch thick. Spread softened butter and sprinkle with cinnamon. Add brown sugar, chopped nuts, and raisins if desired. Roll into a log, cut into portions, place in a greased pan, let rise 45 minutes, then bake at 350°F (175°C) for 15-20 minutes. When cooled slightly, glaze with powdered sugar, melted butter, vanilla extract, and water.
